commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance plus contributor real behavior proof, not a narrow automated code repair.; Cleared: No concrete security or supply-chain concern found; the PR changes Doctor source/tests only and does not touch CI, dependencies, lockfiles, credentials, or release scripts. (sha=6f20c7607ef0a2e31ed092f481ddef46dddad7b2)
commands.review_summary:
The PR extracts Control UI freshness detection into reusable Doctor helpers, registers a core health check with dry-run process effects, filters that check out of the broad structured repair pass, and adds focused tests.

PR surface: Source +107, Tests +186. Total +293 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a bug reproduction: this is a new Doctor health-surface feature. The detector cases are source-testable, but the PR has not shown a high-confidence real CLI run for the changed UI freshness path.
commands.review_followup: The remaining blocker is maintainer acceptance plus contributor real behavior proof, not a narrow automated code repair.

target.title: Doctor: expose UI freshness health findings
target.body:
## Summary

- Exposes Control UI protocol freshness as a structured doctor health check for lint and dry-run repair effects.
- Keeps the real build/rebuild mutation on the existing positional maybeRepairUiProtocolFreshness() path used by openclaw doctor --fix.
- Skips the UI freshness check in the broad structured repair bridge so this PR does not change existing repair ownership or prompt behavior.
- Reports stale UI assets from the mtime mismatch even when git history is unavailable; git log details are treated as optional context only.

This keeps the rule small: the existing UI repair flow now shares its detector with the health registry, and dry-run reports only a process effect when the source checkout can actually build the UI. It intentionally does not invent a file diff for generated UI assets.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
openclaw doctor --lint can report missing/stale Control UI assets, and dry-run repair output can preview the build/rebuild process effect without mutating generated UI files or changing the existing doctor --fix repair ownership.

Real environment tested:
WSL Ubuntu 24.04 durable OpenClaw worktree at /root/src/openclaw-ui-freshness-stack, source harness tests with temporary package roots and no live UI build.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
2. pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
3. git diff --check
4. CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
5. node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-ui-freshness.tsbuildinfo
6. node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-ui-freshness.tsbuildinfo
7. /mnt/c/src/claws-hapi/.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local

Evidence after fix:
Focused Vitest output: 5 test files passed, 45 tests passed. oxfmt reported all matched files use the correct format. oxlint reported 0 warnings and 0 errors. Core and core-test tsgo commands exited 0. Autoreview reported clean: no accepted/actionable findings.

Observed result after fix:
The detector reports missing Control UI assets, maps stale assets to structured findings/effects, skips broad structured mutation for this legacy positional repair, and keeps stale mtime mismatches reportable even when git history is unavailable.

What was not tested:
No live Control UI rebuild was run because this PR intentionally leaves build/rebuild execution on the existing doctor preflight repair path. No generated UI asset diff is emitted because generated build output is not stable or cheap to preview as a file diff.

## Validation

- pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
- pnpm exec oxlint src/commands/doctor-ui.ts src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-conversion-plan.ts
- git diff --check
- CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-ui.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-lint-flow.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-ui-freshness.tsbuildinfo
- node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test-ui-freshness.tsbuildinfo
- autoreview clean
target.author: giodl73-repo
